codeReview.summary: Clean implementation that mirrors existing OpenAI OAuth patterns for Factory.ai. Three new API functions in api.ts follow the same requestJson pattern as their OpenAI counterparts. CredentialsPage.tsx adds the 'Add Factory Key' shortcut button, Factory browser OAuth flow (popup-based), Factory device OAuth flow (with auto-polling), and correctly scopes the shared deviceAuth state by provider. CSS adds tasteful shortcut button styling. All 8 expectedBehavior items are covered and were verified by agent-browser and curl. Two non-blocking observations noted below.

issues.description: Factory browser OAuth popup-close handler calls refreshCredentials() but not refreshQuota(), while the OpenAI equivalent (startBrowserOAuth) calls both. This is likely intentional since Factory doesn't have Codex quota tracking, but creates a subtle inconsistency. If Factory ever adds quota support, this will need updating.

OpenAI and Factory device flows share a single deviceAuth state variable. Starting one flow while the other is active silently overwrites the previous flow's state. This is acceptable for typical single-user usage, but could confuse a user who accidentally starts both flows. A minor UX improvement would be to warn or cancel the previous flow.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker's skillFeedback suggests that for purely frontend/UI features, the 'Write Tests First (Red)' step in the feature-worker skill could note that manual agent-browser verification replaces unit tests when the feature is UI-only with no testable logic. This is a reasonable suggestion — the worker followed the spirit of the skill but couldn't write meaningful unit tests for React UI components in a codebase without a React testing setup (no @testing-library/react, no jsdom).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'For purely frontend features, the Write Tests First (Red) step could note that manual agent-browser verification replaces unit tests when the feature is UI-only with no testable logic'. Worker added 0 tests but verified via 5 interactive agent-browser checks.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Factory browser OAuth popup-close handler omits the refreshQuota() call that the OpenAI browser OAuth handler includes. This asymmetry may reflect a deliberate design choice (Factory has no Codex quota system) but the convention isn't documented anywhere — future workers adding new provider OAuth flows won't know whether to include refreshQuota() or not.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: CredentialsPage.tsx:555 (Factory) only calls refreshCredentials(), while startBrowserOAuth (OpenAI) at line 457 calls both refreshCredentials() and refreshQuota().

summary: Feature passes review. The implementation cleanly adds Factory.ai UI elements (Add Factory Key button, browser OAuth flow, device OAuth flow) by mirroring the existing OpenAI patterns. All 8 expected behaviors were implemented and verified through a combination of pnpm typecheck (pass), pnpm test (221 tests pass), curl API calls, and 5 agent-browser interactive checks. Two non-blocking issues noted: (1) missing refreshQuota() in Factory browser OAuth handler (intentional but creates minor asymmetry), and (2) shared deviceAuth state between providers could cause silent overwrites if both flows are started simultaneously. Neither issue blocks acceptance.
